questions.q: Which guarantee does TCP add that raw IP does not provide?
choices: Reliable, in-order delivery of a byte stream
questions.explain: IP delivers individual packets best-effort. TCP layers on reliability (retransmits losses) and ordering (reassembles segments) to present an ordered byte stream. Encryption is TLS's job; routing and addressing belong to IP.

questions.q: What is the correct sequence of the TCP three-way handshake?
questions.explain: The client sends SYN, the server replies SYN-ACK, and the client confirms with ACK — establishing the connection in one round trip. FIN/RST are used to close or reset, not open.

questions.q: TCP flow control and congestion control solve different problems. Which pairing is correct?
choices: Flow control protects the receiver; congestion control protects the network
Flow control protects the network; congestion control protects the receiver
Both protect only the sender's CPU
Both are handled entirely by IP, not TCP
questions.explain: Flow control uses the receiver's advertised window so the sender doesn't overrun a slow receiver. Congestion control (CUBIC, BBR, …) reacts to loss and RTT so one connection doesn't overwhelm the shared network.
